speaker: CAESAR
text: Contemning Rome, he has done all this and more

speaker: CAESAR
text: In Alexandria. Here’s the manner of ’t:

speaker: CAESAR
text: I’ th’ marketplace, on a tribunal silvered,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Cleopatra and himself in chairs of gold

speaker: CAESAR
text: Were publicly enthroned. At the feet sat

speaker: CAESAR
text: Caesarion, whom they call my father’s son,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And all the unlawful issue that their lust

speaker: CAESAR
text: Since then hath made between them. Unto her

speaker: CAESAR
text: He gave the stablishment of Egypt, made her

speaker: CAESAR
text: Of lower Syria, Cyprus, Lydia,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Absolute queen.

speaker: MAECENAS
text: This in the public eye?

speaker: CAESAR
text: I’ th’ common showplace where they exercise.

speaker: CAESAR
text: His sons he there proclaimed the kings of kings.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Great Media, Parthia, and Armenia

speaker: CAESAR
text: He gave to Alexander; to Ptolemy he assigned

speaker: CAESAR
text: Syria, Cilicia, and Phoenicia. She

speaker: CAESAR
text: In th’ habiliments of the goddess Isis

speaker: CAESAR
text: That day appeared, and oft before gave audience,

speaker: CAESAR
text: As ’tis reported, so.

speaker: MAECENAS
text: Let Rome be thus informed.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Who, queasy with his insolence already,

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Will their good thoughts call from him.

speaker: CAESAR
text: The people knows it and have now received

speaker: CAESAR
text: His accusations.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Who does he accuse?

speaker: CAESAR
text: Caesar, and that, having in Sicily

speaker: CAESAR
text: Sextus Pompeius spoiled, we had not rated him

speaker: CAESAR
text: His part o’ th’ isle. Then does he say he lent me

speaker: CAESAR
text: Some shipping, unrestored. Lastly, he frets

speaker: CAESAR
text: That Lepidus of the triumvirate

speaker: CAESAR
text: Should be deposed and, being, that we detain

speaker: CAESAR
text: All his revenue.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Sir, this should be answered.

speaker: CAESAR
text: ’Tis done already, and the messenger gone.

speaker: CAESAR
text: I have told him Lepidus was grown too cruel,

speaker: CAESAR
text: That he his high authority abused

speaker: CAESAR
text: And did deserve his change. For what I have conquered,

speaker: CAESAR
text: I grant him part; but then in his Armenia

speaker: CAESAR
text: And other of his conquered kingdoms I

speaker: CAESAR
text: Demand the like.

speaker: MAECENAS
text: He’ll never yield to that.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Nor must not then be yielded to in this.

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: Hail, Caesar, and my lord! Hail, most dear Caesar.

speaker: CAESAR
text: That ever I should call thee castaway!

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: You have not called me so, nor have you cause.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Why have you stol’n upon us thus? You come not

speaker: CAESAR
text: Like Caesar’s sister. The wife of Antony

speaker: CAESAR
text: Should have an army for an usher and

speaker: CAESAR
text: The neighs of horse to tell of her approach

speaker: CAESAR
text: Long ere she did appear. The trees by th’ way

speaker: CAESAR
text: Should have borne men, and expectation fainted,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Longing for what it had not. Nay, the dust

speaker: CAESAR
text: Should have ascended to the roof of heaven,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Raised by your populous troops. But you are come

speaker: CAESAR
text: A market-maid to Rome, and have prevented

speaker: CAESAR
text: The ostentation of our love, which, left unshown,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Is often left unloved. We should have met you

speaker: CAESAR
text: By sea and land, supplying every stage

speaker: CAESAR
text: With an augmented greeting.

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: Good my lord,

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: To come thus was I not constrained, but did it

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: On my free will. My lord, Mark Antony,

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: Hearing that you prepared for war, acquainted

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: My grievèd ear withal, whereon I begged

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: His pardon for return.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Which soon he granted,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Being an abstract ’tween his lust and him.

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: Do not say so, my lord.

speaker: CAESAR
text: I have eyes upon him,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And his affairs come to me on the wind.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Where is he now?

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: My lord, in Athens.

speaker: CAESAR
text: No, my most wrongèd sister. Cleopatra

speaker: CAESAR
text: Hath nodded him to her. He hath given his empire

speaker: CAESAR
text: Up to a whore, who now are levying

speaker: CAESAR
text: The kings o’ th’ Earth for war. He hath assembled

speaker: CAESAR
text: Bocchus, the King of Libya; Archelaus

text: Of Cappadocia; Philadelphos, King

text: Of Paphlagonia; the Thracian king, Adallas;

text: King Manchus of Arabia; King of Pont;

text: Herod of Jewry; Mithridates, King

text: Of Comagen; Polemon and Amyntas,

speaker: CAESAR

text: With a more larger list of scepters.

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: Ay me, most wretched,

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: That have my heart parted betwixt two friends

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: That does afflict each other!

speaker: CAESAR
text: Welcome hither.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Your letters did withhold our breaking forth

speaker: CAESAR
text: Till we perceived both how you were wrong led

speaker: CAESAR
text: And we in negligent danger. Cheer your heart.

speaker: CAESAR
text: Be you not troubled with the time, which drives

speaker: CAESAR
text: O’er your content these strong necessities,

speaker: CAESAR
text: But let determined things to destiny

speaker: CAESAR
text: Hold unbewailed their way. Welcome to Rome,

speaker: CAESAR
text: Nothing more dear to me. You are abused

speaker: CAESAR
text: Beyond the mark of thought, and the high gods,

speaker: CAESAR
text: To do you justice, makes his ministers

speaker: CAESAR
text: Of us and those that love you. Best of comfort,

speaker: CAESAR
text: And ever welcome to us.

speaker: AGRIPPA
text: Welcome, lady.

speaker: MAECENAS
text: Welcome, dear madam.

speaker: MAECENAS
text: Each heart in Rome does love and pity you;

speaker: MAECENAS
text: Only th’ adulterous Antony, most large

speaker: MAECENAS
text: In his abominations, turns you off

speaker: MAECENAS
text: And gives his potent regiment to a trull

speaker: MAECENAS
text: That noises it against us.

speaker: OCTAVIA
text: Is it so, sir?

speaker: CAESAR
text: Most certain. Sister, welcome. Pray you

speaker: CAESAR
text: Be ever known to patience. My dear’st sister!
